name: Halawa
desc: CDP between Aiea and Salt Lake — includes Halawa Heights, working-class residential, freeway-adjacent convenience.
insider: Halawa Heights above has better views and quieter streets than the valley floor — worth the winding drive up
Proximity to both H-1 and H-3 makes this one of the most commuter-connected spots on Oahu — you can reach Kaneohe in 15 minutes via tunnel
The New Aloha Stadium Entertainment District is a massive redevelopment project that will transform lower Halawa over the next decade
The valley floor floods in heavy rains — seriously check FEMA flood zone maps if you're buying a home down low
Halawa District Park has a well-maintained community pool that's way less crowded than beach parks
The Halawa Xeriscape Garden near the stadium is a free hidden gem showcasing drought-tolerant landscaping perfect for Hawaii yards
Red Lobster and Zippy's near the stadium are the go-to spots — not fancy but they're the neighborhood standbys
The H-3 freeway on-ramp here is the fastest way to the Windward side, but the Tetsuo Harano Tunnels close occasionally for maintenance — check before you go
Military families from nearby bases make up a significant portion of renters — expect some turnover in neighbors every few years
Airport noise is noticeable in lower Halawa, especially early morning — upper Halawa Heights is mostly above the flight path
